Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to craft a DuckDB SQL query that filters cases based on event count The table is event_log with columns case_id activity timestamp and possibly others We want to return all events from cases that have at most six events i e six or fewer

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So we need to compute per case event counts filter to those with count <= 6 and then join back to original rows or use a window function

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: One typical approach using a subquery or CTE to compute counts per case_id then inner join on case_id where count <= 6 Or use a WHERE case_id IN SELECT case_id FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id HAVING COUNT <= 6 Thats straightforward

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Alternatively using a window function SELECT FROM SELECT COUNT OVER PARTITION BY case_id as case_event_count FROM event_log WHERE case_event_count <= 6 That works

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We need to ensure to include all columns Probably SELECT FROM event_log WHERE case_id IN SELECT case_id FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id HAVING COUNT <= 6 is simpler But both are fine

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: DuckDB is a SQL engine its SQL is standard So any standard SQL works

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I think the user wants us to craft the SQL query as requested assuming we are the model being benchmarked

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Thus Ill provide a clear answer the DuckDB SQL query that solves the problem Ill choose the WHERE IN approach
